input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest .NET 8 SDK)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android + Windows)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK requires the Windows SDK and C++ build tools:

```powershell
# Option A: Full Visual Studio (recommended)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then add required components via VS Installer, OR command-line:
# (run elevated)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet

# Option B: Standalone Windows SDK via winget
winget search "Windows SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsSDK.10.0.22621
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload bundles Android SDK management, but you need to accept licenses and may need Java:

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (Android builds need JDK 17)
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify Java
java --version

# The Android SDK is auto-managed by the dotnet workload.
# Default location:
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
Test-Path $androidSdkPath

# If you need to set ANDROID_HOME explicitly:
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdkPath, "User")

# Accept Android licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# List installed Android components
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Install a specific platform/emulator if needed (discover available versions):
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;"
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"
```

## 5. Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V is enabled (required for hardware acceleration)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All

# Enable if needed (reboot required)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

## 6. Create & Verify a MAUI Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## 7. Full Diagnostic Check

```powershell
# Comprehensive environment doctor
dotnet workload update
dotnet nuget locals all --list

# If issues arise, repair workloads
dotnet workload repair
```

---

**Key version notes:** Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you install .NET 9 instead. The Windows TFM (`net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) must match your installed Windows SDK — check with `dotnet --list-sdks` and `dir "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include\"` to discover available versions.
